researched_category.notes: Discord is a voice, video, and text communication platform for communities and teams. Its developer platform focuses on building bots and apps that extend Discord servers.

one_liner.value: A voice, video, and text communication platform for communities and teams, with a broad public API for building bots, apps, and integrations.
one_liner.notes: Official Discord Developer Platform overview describes building bots and integrations on Discord.

methods.notes: Discord supports two authentication methods: (1) Bot tokens (sent as 'Authorization: Bot TOKEN') which act as bearer tokens for bot accounts, and (2) OAuth2 bearer tokens (sent as 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN') for user-authorized access. The API reference states: 'Authenticating with the Discord API can be done in one of two ways: 1. Using a bot token... 2. Using an OAuth2 bearer token gained through the OAuth2 API.' Bot tokens are the most common approach for programmatic agent toolkits.

access.notes: Any Discord user can create applications and get bot tokens from the Developer Portal (https://discord.com/developers/applications) for free. No paid plan, credit card, or admin approval is required. Multiple sources confirm Discord charges nothing for creating applications, generating bot tokens, or using the API.

summary.value: Discord provides a well-documented public REST API (https://discord.com/api) with broad coverage including channels, messages, guilds (servers), users, roles, permissions, voice, webhooks, applications, entitlements, commands, and more. It also has a Gateway WebSocket API for real-time event streaming. The API docs index 40+ resource categories. The Composio Discord toolkit exposes 28 tools, indicating a rich API surface.

agent_interface.notes: No official Discord MCP server exists, but several community MCP servers are available (e.g., v-3/discordmcp on GitHub with 219 stars, barryyip0625/mcp-discord on npm/Smithery). Composio also provides a Discord toolkit with 28 tools and 1 trigger via its MCP Tool Router.

rationale.value: Discord is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a well-documented public REST API with broad coverage, self-serve free credential access (anyone with a Discord account can create applications and get bot tokens), multiple authentication options (bot tokens or OAuth2), and existing community MCP servers and Composio toolkit integrations. No paid plan, credit card, or admin approval is required. The only minor consideration is that some advanced OAuth2 scopes require partner approval, but the core bot API (which covers messaging, guild management, permissions, etc.) is fully accessible to any developer.

s1.quote: Build bots and integrations on Discord, or connect your game with rich presence, voice chat, and more.
s1.title: Discord Developer Platform

s2.quote: The Discord API is a REST API that allows you to interact with Discord data from your own applications. It's the primary way to interact with Discord from your own code.
s2.title: API Reference - Discord Developer Docs

s3.quote: Authenticating with the Discord API can be done in one of two ways: 1. Using a bot token found on the Bot page within your app's settings. 2. Using an OAuth2 bearer token gained through the OAuth2 API.
s3.title: API Reference - Authentication

s4.quote: OAuth2 enables application developers to build applications that utilize authentication and data from the Discord API. Within Discord, there are multiple types of OAuth2 authentication.
s4.title: OAuth2 - Discord Developer Docs

s5.quote: Discord charges nothing for creating applications, generating bot tokens, or using the API. There are no paid tiers, no credit card required, and no rate limit tiers based on payment.
s5.title: How to Get Your Discord Bot Token

s6.quote: First, you'll need to create an app in the developer portal if you don't have one already. Create App. Enter a name for your app, then press Create.
s6.title: Building your first Discord Bot

s7.quote: Discord - An instant messaging and VoIP social platform. Category: team chat. Auth: OAUTH2. Tools: 28. Triggers: 1.
s7.title: Discord - Composio Toolkit

s8.quote: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that enables LLMs to interact with Discord channels, allowing them to send and read messages through Discord's API.
s8.title: v-3/discordmcp - Discord MCP Server

s9.quote: The Discord MCP server is an implementation of the Model Context Protocol that connects your AI agent and assistants like Claude, Cursor, etc directly to your Discord account.
s9.title: Discord MCP Integration for AI Agents - Composio

limitations: Some advanced OAuth2 scopes require partner approval from Discord, but they are not needed for the core bot API which covers the vast majority of use cases.
